source_jobs.title: DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
DevOps Engineer



Salary Banding: HUF 1M - HUF 1.5 per Month
Contract: Full-Time, Permanent
Working Location: Hybrid, 2 Days a week in the office, minimum
Office Location: Budapest


Join My Team as a DevOps Engineer at Hawk-Eye Innovations
Hi, I’m Dan Pina, the DevOps Team Lead at Hawk-Eye Innovations, and I’m looking for a DevOps Engineer to join our growing team. If you’re passionate about cloud infrastructure, automation, and working on cutting-edge sports technology, this could be a great opportunity for you.



What Your Day Will Look Like
As a DevOps Engineer, you’ll be hands-on with our cloud infrastructure, automation, and deployment pipelines. You’ll work alongside experienced engineers, learning from the best while contributing to key projects. Your day might include:

 - Building and maintaining infrastructure for large-scale data and video streaming platforms.

 - Supporting development teams by making deployments easier, faster, and more reliable.

 - Improving security by helping to monitor and prevent potential risks.

 - Enhancing observability through monitoring, logging, and alerting tools.

 - Collaborating with engineers across teams to ensure our DevOps culture thrives.
   
   

What We Value
We’re all about collaboration, learning, and innovation. Whether you’re fresh into DevOps or have a little experience under your belt, we’re looking for someone who:

 - Is eager to learn and grow in cloud technologies.

 - Enjoys solving problems and automating repetitive tasks.

 - Works well in a fast-paced, tech-driven environment.

 - Communicates clearly and thrives in a team setting.
   
   

Tech Stack and Skill Requirements
We don’t expect you to know everything, but some experience or familiarity with the following will help:

 - Cloud Platforms: AWS

 - Infrastructure as Code: Terraform, Cloudformation, Ansible

 - CI/CD Pipelines: GitHub Actions

 - Containers: Docker

 - Security Principles and basic networking concepts

 - Version Control: Git

 - Message Brokers: RabbitMQ, Pulsar, AMQ (a plus)

 - Monitoring & Logging: Grafana, Elastic, Datadog (a plus)



Benefits & Perks

 - Annual leave per local labour law

 - Hybrid working (2 days/week in office)

 - Sony Group perks – discounted tickets and events

 - Cafeteria options (SZÉP Card, nursery/kindergarten support, MOL Bubi Pass)

 - Private medical, life & accident insurance, dental discounts

 - Seniority awards, Employee Assistance Program

 - “All You Can Move” Pass option
   
   

Equal Opportunity Employer

We’re committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace. Everyone is treated fairly, regardless of gender, race, religion, age, disability, or background. We want you to be your best and truest self here.



Apply Today

If you’re excited by the idea of solving real-world problems at scale and want to make a difference in the world of sports tech, we’d love to hear from you.

Join Our Team as a Senior Java Engineer at Hawk-Eye Innovations

I’m excited to invite you to apply for the Senior Java Engineer position in our Data and Platforms Value Stream at Hawk-Eye Innovations. If you're passionate about building scalable backend systems and want to work on high-impact projects for sports around the world, this could be the ideal role for you.



What Your Day Will Look Like

As a Senior Java Engineer in our team, you'll play a key role in designing, developing, and optimizing data-intensive backend services that power live events. Our team is responsible for managing data feeds, event-driven processing, and cloud-based infrastructure to deliver real-time insights to global leagues and partners.

You’ll work in an agile environment, collaborating with engineers across our backend team. Given the high-profile nature of our work, we operate in a fast-moving environment where efficiency and scalability are key to success.

At Hawk-Eye, we believe in collaboration, ownership, and innovation. We strive to create an environment where engineers can contribute meaningfully, solve challenging problems, and improve their skills. Given the pace of work, we value engineers who can hit the ground running, take initiative, and contribute to mission-critical projects from day one.

 - Develop, optimise, and maintain backend services using Java (Spring Boot)

 - Contribute to design decisions for new and existing projects

 - Ensure system reliability and performance in high-traffic, real-time environments



Tech Stack and Skill Requirements

Required:

 - Strong Java 17 & Spring Boot experience

 - Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS preferred)

 - Familiarity with observability tools (e.g., Datadog, Prometheus, OpenTelemetry)

 - Experience with microservices and distributed systems

 - Understanding of monitoring, logging, and traceability

 - Ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced environment

Bonus Skills:

 - Experience with event-driven architectures (Kafka, RabbitMQ, SQS, etc.)

 - Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines and automation (GitHub Actions, Terraform)

 - Familiarity with containerisation and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes)

 - Exposure to real-time data processing and sports tech

source_jobs.title: Senior Computer Vision Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR COMPUTER VISION ENGINEER
JOIN OUR TEAM AT HAWK-EYE INNOVATIONS


VALUE STREAM - TRACKING & AUTO OFFICIATING



LOCATION: HYBRID WORKING – MINIMUM 2 DAYS PER WEEK IN THE OFFICE
OFFICE LOCATIONS: BASINGSTOKE, LONDON OR BRISTOL
CONTRACT: PERMANENT, FULL-TIME
SALARY: £65,440 – £87,260

Hi, I’m James Sharam, Head of Computer Vision at Hawk-Eye Innovations. I’m looking for a Senior Computer Vision Engineer to join our growing team. This is a key role focused on real-time skeletal, ball and game object tracking for multiple sports. A strong background in mathematics and modelling is encouraged. If that kind of technical challenge excites you, I’d love to hear from you.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Senior Computer Vision Engineer – Algorithms to join our Computer Vision team at Hawk-Eye Innovations.

This role is suited to an experienced engineer who enjoys owning complex algorithmic systems and seeing them operate in real-world, production environments.

The role is strongly algorithm-focused, with an emphasis on accuracy, performance, robustness, and system-level thinking, spanning modern C++, multi-camera geometry, cross-platform deployments, and cloud-enabled pipelines.



WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Designing, developing, and improving ball and player tracking algorithms using multi-camera systems

 - Owning and evolving multi-view geometry solutions for accurate, court-wide tracking

 - Developing robust approaches to occlusion handling, recovery, and failure modes in fast-paced sports scenarios

 - Enhancing and maintaining mature, long-running computer vision systems used in live production

 - Optimising algorithms for performance and reliability, including CPU and GPU considerations

 - Contributing to cross-platform systems, ensuring algorithms run consistently across environments

 - Supporting the integration of algorithms into cloud-enabled and distributed systems

 - Working in an agile environment to deliver robust, production-ready solutions at scale


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the development and improvement of computer vision algorithms from concept through to production

 - Design, implement, test, and validate complex algorithmic features

 - Take ownership of algorithmic components and drive their long-term maintainability and technical quality

 - Collaborate closely with engineers across computer vision, tracking, tooling, and platform teams

 - Contribute to technical discussions around architecture, performance, scalability, and deployment

 - Identify weaknesses in existing approaches and propose pragmatic improvements

 - Share progress, decisions, and learnings with the wider team

 - Support and mentor other engineers through code reviews and technical guidance




SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE, AND EXPERIENCE

We’re looking for someone with:

 - Strong commercial experience developing computer vision algorithms

 - Advanced knowledge of modern C++, working within large, production codebases

 - Strong understanding of performance optimisation, including CPU and GPU workloads

 - Solid experience with image processing, geometry, and multi-camera systems

 - Experience working with cross-platform systems and real-world deployment environments

 - Familiarity integrating algorithmic components into cloud-based or distributed systems

 - Strong debugging skills and the ability to reason about complex system behaviour

 - Confident use of Git and Visual Studio in collaborative engineering environments

Nice to have (but not essential):

 - Experience with CUDA or GPU acceleration

 - Awareness of concurrency, networking, or operating system concepts

 - Experience deploying or supporting algorithms in cloud environments

 - Interest in machine learning techniques and related libraries




REWARD, BENEFITS, AND WELLNESS


UK BENEFITS

 - 25 days annual leave (excluding bank holidays)

 - Enhanced pension scheme with 5% matching

 - Hybrid working model

 - Complimentary Unmind wellbeing app

 - Onsite gym

JOIN OUR TEAM AS A PRODUCT OWNER AT HAWK-EYE INNOVATIONS


Hi, I’m Alison Marr, Product Lead for the Tracking and Auto Officiating Value Stream at Hawk-Eye Innovations. Are you interested in working with the latest technologies in a fast-paced environment, with exciting career opportunities in a growing business?

At Hawk-Eye, we build technology that powers some of the most exciting and critical moments in sport, from instant replays and broadcast augmentation to officiating systems trusted by leagues, federations, broadcasters, and millions of fans worldwide.

Our Value Stream delivers the tracking and automatic officiating services that have revolutionized global sport. We are looking for an experienced, highly autonomous Product Owner to take full ownership of a major product, platform, or technology across its complete lifecycle. At Level 4, you will step beyond day-to-day backlog management to actively shape short-to-medium-range roadmaps, define strategic OKRs, and manage complex stakeholder relationships with some of the biggest names in elite global sports. We need a technical, highly organised leader who can navigate ambiguity, make high-impact trade-off decisions, and drive consistency across our product portfolio while ensuring our solutions exceed the highest standards and strict customer SLAs.


What You'll Be Doing

 - Stakeholder Management & Ambiguity: Champion your product as its primary global ambassador. Lead high-level technical and business discussions, align cross-functional engineering teams, and successfully navigate complex, ambiguous environments to keep deliveries on track.

 - Quality & Built-In Engineering Excellence: Collaborate with Development Leads, Solution Architects, and QA to enforce common architectural patterns. Ensure built-in quality at every stage to completely eliminate the cost of delays (CoDs) associated with rework or recalls.

 - Operational Readiness & 3rd Line Support: Define success metrics and monitor operational data to guide business decisions. Oversee 3rd-line support within your product scope and guarantee that training, documentation, and testing are executed flawlessly ahead of rollout into BAU.


What We're Looking For

 - 2–3+ years of experience as a Product Manager or Product Owner, demonstrating growing autonomy in managing all stages of the product lifecycle and leading short-range roadmap definition.

 - Advanced stakeholder management skills with a proven ability to align diverse cross-functional engineering teams, product leads, and high-profile external clients.

 - Strong technical grounding with concrete experience managing technically complex products and cloud-based data solutions.

 - Strategic product execution experience, particularly in defining Minimum Marketable Features (MMFs), Minimum Viable Products (MVPs), and setting clear OKRs.

 - Superb leadership and communication skills with a knack for removing product-related obstacles, conducting trade-off analyses, and keeping priorities visible.

source_jobs.description:
Head of Youth & Amateur Products



About Hawk-Eye Innovations

 

Hawk-Eye Innovations, a Sony Sports Technology company, is the technology behind sport's biggest moments. From electronic line calling at Wimbledon and the US Open, to VAR, Goal-Line Technology and Semi-Automated Offside in football, ball tracking in cricket, the TMO in rugby, replay in the NFL, NHL and NBA, and Statcast in MLB, when the world's biggest events need to make the right call, capture the perfect angle, or unlock the next layer of insight, they trust Hawk-Eye. Since 2001 we've been making sport fairer, safer, more engaging and better informed, fusing computer vision, AI and broadcast craft across 25 sports in 100+ countries. Pioneering. Inspiring. Innovating in sport, from the field to the fan.

Google Workspace to M365 and M365 to M365 Migrations

Role Overview

Reporting to the IT Director, this role provides the technical leadership and hands-on delivery required to continue a multi-phase collaboration platform consolidation programme across the group. The programme is migrating several business units onto a single Microsoft 365 tenant, retiring legacy Google Workspace and standalone Microsoft 365 environments along the way.

You will be responsible for overseeing in-flight migrations, planning and executing future migrations, and decommissioning environments that have already been transitioned. The work spans Microsoft 365 tenant-to-tenant migrations, Google Workspace to Microsoft 365 migrations, and the coexistence, cutover and stabilisation activities that surround them.

Key Responsibilities

Migration Delivery

 - Take ownership of in-flight and upcoming platform migrations, including both Microsoft 365 to Microsoft 365 (tenant consolidation) and Google Workspace Enterprise to Microsoft 365 transitions.

 - Design and execute migration approaches for mail, calendars, contacts, files, chat and identity, using the Microsoft Migration Toolset (Exchange Online migration, SharePoint and OneDrive migration tooling) and complementary third-party tooling where appropriate.

 - Manage coexistence periods where dual systems operate in parallel, including mail flow configuration, calendar interop, free/busy visibility, and delta synchronisation.

 - Plan and execute cutover activities including domain MX changes, primary SMTP swaps, UPN transitions, and the controlled retirement of source environments.

 - Identify migration risks, edge cases and dependencies, including shared mailboxes, delegations, calendar resource booking, SaaS authentication dependencies, and cross-document references such as Google Sheets IMPORTRANGE.

 - Document findings and recommend approaches to address them.

Identity, Licensing and Coexistence

 - Plan and implement identity strategies that accommodate Entra ID, Google federation and the broader SaaS application landscape during transitions.

 - Manage licensing transitions across Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace, including Cloud Identity downgrades for retained identity-only accounts.

 - Maintain mail flow integrity across domains during coexistence, including accepted domain types, forwarding configurations, primary SMTP and alias management.

Tenant Decommissioning

 - Oversee the controlled decommissioning of source environments after successful migration, covering data retention, identity downgrade, licence release and tenant retirement.

 - Coordinate retention of legacy native content as a safety net where required.

 - Manage SaaS application transitions from legacy identity providers to Entra ID single sign-on.

Stakeholder Engagement and Proposal Support

Hardware Technician - Tennis



🎾 Hawk-Eye Tennis

📍 Location: Basingstoke - Approximately 80% Office, 20% on-site (local & global)

📝 Contract: Permanent, Full Time

💰 Salary Band: £26,990 - £33,040 Depending on Experience




WHY HAWK-EYE & WHY TENNIS?

Join us at Hawk-Eye and be part of an exhilarating journey through the world of sports events. Our operations teams play a crucial role in delivering high-quality experiences at major sporting events worldwide. These are the different areas that you can explore:

 - Technical: Testing, improving and stress testing our systems to enhance the performance of our delivery across the tours.

 - Creative: Working closely with TV directors and external broadcasters to provide statistics and storytelling of insights across tennis matches during live broadcasts.

 - Operations: Reviewing, improving and strategising how we improve the efficiency of delivery across the globe.

 - Projects: On-site management of our service delivery across the globe. Liaising with external stakeholders to organise our technical requirements to be ready and sites to be prepared for our arrival, and to deliver our services to the deadline of the events.

 - Broadcast Production: Rigging and directing live TV broadcasts across the Masters 1000s ATP tour.

 

» Specific Job Overview

This is a unique hybrid role that will see you acting as the vital link between the Hardware Team and the Onsite Operations Team. With responsibilities overlapping both hardware support and system operations, you will play a central role in ensuring cohesion between these two pillars of the department. When onsite at tournaments, you won’t just contribute to the success of the event, you’ll also be leading the way in how we manage and deploy kit, helping to refine our build-out processes and create a more streamlined, integrated operation.



Your time will be split between our Basingstoke HQ and international tennis event, with the balance depending on the operational demands of the tennis calendar. While the schedule will ebb and flow throughout the year, your flexibility and ability to adapt to the rhythm of the tour will be key.



You will be part of the Tennis Operations Team, working closely with the Projects, Warehouse, and Logistics Teams. The Operations Team is responsible for planning, maintaining, and delivering the resources required to execute events at the highest possible standard, ensuring the right staff and equipment are in the right place at the right time.



The Tennis Department manages over £2 million worth of equipment across 200+ flight cases, deployed globally. With more than 30 tons of equipment moving across continents, tennis is one of the most logistically complex departments in the company.

source_jobs.title: Product Owner
source_jobs.description:
JOIN OUR TEAM AS A PRODUCT OWNER AT HAWK-EYE INNOVATIONS


Hi, I’m Alison Marr, Product Lead for the Tracking and Auto Officiating Value Stream at Hawk-Eye Innovations. Are you interested in working with the latest technologies in a fast-paced environment, with exciting career opportunities in a growing business?

At Hawk-Eye, we build technology that powers some of the most exciting and critical moments in sport, from instant replays and broadcast augmentation to officiating systems trusted by leagues, federations, broadcasters, and millions of fans worldwide.

Our Value Stream delivers the tracking and automatic officiating services that have revolutionized global sport. From our world-renowned ball and object tracking systems to SkeleTRACK, our real-time skeletal tracking tech that powers Semi-Automated Offside Technology (SAOT) and electronic line-calling we turn live athletic movement into precise, high-stakes decisions and elite data insights.

We are looking for an experienced, highly autonomous Product Owner to take full ownership of a major product, platform, or technology across its complete lifecycle. At Level 4, you will step beyond day-to-day backlog management to actively shape short-to-medium-range roadmaps, define strategic OKRs, and manage complex stakeholder relationships with some of the biggest names in elite global sports. We need a technical, highly organised leader who can navigate ambiguity, make high-impact trade-off decisions, and drive consistency across our product portfolio while ensuring our solutions exceed the highest standards and strict customer SLAs.


What You'll Be Doing

 - Strategic Backlog & Scope Ownership: Serve as the definitive voice of the user, client, and business. Fully own the team backlog, defining epics, features, and user stories—and proactively handle complex dependencies and time-critical priorities to maximise ROI.

 - Roadmap, Vision & Market Strategy: Actively contribute to and shape the strategic roadmap and vision for our tracking portfolio. Research the market, track competitors, and perform deep user analysis to proactively propose improvements to system reliability, scalability, and UX.

 - Stakeholder Management & Ambiguity: Champion your product as its primary global ambassador. Lead high-level technical and business discussions, align cross-functional engineering teams, and successfully navigate complex, ambiguous environments to keep deliveries on track.

 - Quality & Built-In Engineering Excellence: Collaborate with Development Leads, Solution Architects, and QA to enforce common architectural patterns. Ensure built-in quality at every stage to completely eliminate the cost of delays (CoDs) associated with rework or recalls.

 - Operational Readiness & 3rd Line Support: Define success metrics and monitor operational data to guide business decisions. Oversee 3rd-line support within your product scope and guarantee that training, documentation, and testing are executed flawlessly ahead of rollout into BAU.


What We're Looking For

 - 2–3+ years of experience as a Product Manager or Product Owner, demonstrating growing autonomy in managing all stages of the product lifecycle and leading short-range roadmap definition.

 - Advanced stakeholder management skills with a proven ability to align diverse cross-functional engineering teams, product leads, and high-profile external clients.

 - Strong technical grounding with concrete experience managing technically complex products and cloud-based data solutions.

 - Strategic product execution experience, particularly in defining Minimum Marketable Features (MMFs), Minimum Viable Products (MVPs), and setting clear OKRs.

 - Superb leadership and communication skills with a knack for removing product-related obstacles, conducting trade-off analyses, and keeping priorities visible.

Bonus Points For

 - Experience working on high-performance products within a business-to-business (B2B) setting.

 - Experience with real-t

source_jobs.title: Match Operations Assistant (TG & RRA) - Croatia
source_jobs.description:
Location: Onsite at stadiums in Croatia

Contract: Consultant, Part-Time

Salary: Up to €180 per day

Training date: 13.7.26-16.7.26 In Zagreb




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Match Operations Assistant, you will be responsible for setting up and operating our cutting-edge systems at football stadiums throughout Europe for various prestigious competitions, including the 1.HNL, UEFA Champions League, UEFA Europa League, and International European Qualification matches.

We will provide you with full technical training, support, and direct exposure to our technology to perform your match-day tasks. This role is a fantastic gateway to encourage your professional development and career progression within Hawk-Eye Innovations.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Match Operations Assistants manage a variety of critical technical tasks on a match day, depending on the specific role assigned. Your duties will include:

 - Technical Setup: Setting up, configuring, and troubleshooting stadium communication systems.

 - Official Support: Working directly with the Referee Review Area Monitor (RRA) to support match officials.

 - Pre-Match Integrity: Conducting thorough pre-match checks and liaising closely with stadium personnel and officials.

 - Live System Operations: Monitoring and operating technical systems seamlessly throughout the live match.

 - Logistics Coordination: Managing equipment and coordinating logistics for travelling between stadium locations.




SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE, AND EXPERTISE

Essentials:

 - Excellent organisational, analytical, and problem-solving skills

 - Proactive, responsible attitude, able to work independently with minimal supervision

 - Time management skills and ability to work under pressure in a live broadcast environment

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills, proficient in English

 - Flexibility in adapting to irregular working hours

Desirables:

 - Technical background, ideally in IT or Broadcast

 - Interest in sports, especially football

 - Experience with VT/replay technology or live event/technical production

 - Willingness to travel locally and, if required, further afield in Europe

 - Driving licence
   


WHAT DOES A TYPICAL DAY LOOK LIKE?

For a typical kick off you can expect our call time to be 5 to 6 hours prior to the start of the match. Throughout the day you will be faced with several hard deadlines in which you will have to meet in order for the service to run effectively and efficiently.

Your day will generally span three main phases:

 1. Pre-Match Calibration & Tech Setup: Spend the first part of your day testing communications, calibrating equipment, and optimising the setup. You will ensure the entire system is fully prepped and ready 2 hours prior to kick-off for the match officials to test its integrity.

 2. The Warm-Up Phase: Utilise the teams' warm-up period as your final opportunity to conduct thorough live checks, ensuring the system and all comms are 100% functional before going live.

 3. Live Match Operations: Monitor the system and communications in real-time throughout the live match, ensuring seamless connectivity, high efficiency, and instant troubleshooting if any issues arise.




WHAT CHALLENGES MIGHT I FACE?

While this role offers a unique entry point into sports technology, it comes with specific operational demands. Please consider these factors before submitting your application:

 - Flexible Working Hours: Due to the nature of the sports industry, there is often work on weekends and midweek evenings, and we are expected to fulfil our roles until the last kick.

 - High-Pressure Situations: Working in a live broadcast environment on a production that is distributed globally and widely seen by many will bring levels of pressure.

These factors offer different challenges daily as an operator. We will do everything we can to train you and support you to the best possible level, but these factors should be considered before submitting your application.




TEAM

source_jobs.title: Delivery Manager
source_jobs.description:
DELIVERY MANAGER



Location: London, Basingstoke, Bristol
Hybrid Working - 2 Days in the office
Salary: £42,000 - £66,000




Hi, I'm Zoe Evans, Senior Delivery Manager at Hawk-Eye Innovations in the Tracking and Auto Officiating Value Stream, and we're looking for a Delivery Manager to join our growing technology unit.

This is an exciting opportunity to join one of the core pillars of Hawk-Eye Innovations. Since our inception in 2001, we have been a leading provider of advanced officiating and tracking tools for elite sports. Our team spearheads the creation of sophisticated optical tracking systems, managing an end-to-end remit that encompasses everything from initial camera capture to the delivery of real-time data.

You will be joining a collaborative, cross-functional Delivery Team that partners directly with Product, Software Engineering, and Operations. Unlike traditional delivery roles tied to a single silo, you will orchestrate the seamless execution of diverse initiatives across multiple squads and solution teams, helping us provide both integrated services and fully automated officiating technology to the biggest names in the global sports industry.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for someone who enjoys bringing people together, solving complex problems, and helping highly skilled technical teams deliver their best work.

As a Delivery Manager, you will underpin the timing, flow, and execution coordination within our value stream. Working alongside Product Managers, Engineering Leads, and Operations, you will lead multiple projects that accomplish key company objectives, whether that’s launching a new product, integrating existing offerings into new markets, or improving our core technical foundations for future success.

This isn’t a traditional, rigid project management role. We operate in a fast-paced, reactive environment where priorities can shift as new opportunities emerge. We need someone who can quickly assess impacts, manage uncertainty, and build capability in a growing business, all while ensuring our teams have the environment they need to thrive.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Leading project delivery across multiple tracking and automated officiating initiatives to tight deadlines.

 - Coordinating work cross-functionally across multiple engineering squads, product teams, and operational units.

 - Managing delivery roadmaps, timelines, milestones, and project planning activities.

 - Driving agile maturity and coaching best practices within the value stream to foster a continuous delivery culture.

 - Tracking and communicating timelines to the wider business, ensuring stakeholders have clear visibility of project status.

 - Identifying dependency collisions, delivery risks, and sequencing issues early to implement mitigation measures.

 - Utilizing data to your advantage by measuring the success of outcomes and identifying areas for process optimization.

 - Building key relationships both internally and externally to facilitate organizational change and transformation.
   




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

Your first few months will focus on learning our sophisticated optical tracking systems, understanding how our cross-functional squads interact, and building strong relationships across the business.

As your confidence grows, you will become the go-to person for implementing and communicating project best practices throughout the product lifecycle, from idea to execution. Supported by the Head of Delivery and VS Delivery Leads, you will take on increasingly complex projects and play an active role in the leadership of the wider value stream.

Success in this role means establishing yourself as a trusted partner who anticipates emerging issues ahead of time, keeps work moving efficiently, and empowers our technical teams to deliver world-class sports technology.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Coordinate the successful delivery of multiple cross-functional projects aligned with company priorities.

source_jobs.title: Computer Vision Engineer - Algorithms
source_jobs.description:
Computer Vision Engineer – Algorithms
Value Stream - Tracking & Auto Officiating

Join Our Team at Hawk-Eye Innovations
Location: Hybrid working – minimum 2 days per week in the office
Office locations: Budapest, Váci út 144-150, 1138 Hungary
Contract: Permanent, Full-Time
Salary: HUF 1,045,000 – 1,567,500 gross per month

About the Role
Hi, I’m Thomas Rychlik, Discipline Lead for Computer Vision Algorithms at Hawk-Eye Innovations. I’m looking for a Computer Vision Engineer – Algorithms to join our Computer Vision team. This role is ideal for someone with early commercial or strong academic experience in computer vision who wants to continue developing their algorithmic skills while working on real-world, production-grade sports technology.

You’ll be working on elite-level tracking systems across sports such as football and tennis, contributing directly to the development and improvement of core computer vision algorithms used in live sporting environments. The role is strongly algorithm-focused, with an emphasis on problem solving, performance, and accuracy.

What You’ll Work On

 - Developing and improving football ball and player tracking algorithms using multi-camera systems

 - Contributing to multi-view geometry solutions for accurate tracking across the field of play

 - Supporting approaches to handling occlusion and recovery in fast-paced sports scenarios

 - Enhancing existing, mature computer vision systems used in live production

 - Optimising algorithms with performance and reliability in mind, including CPU and GPU considerations

 - Working in an agile environment to deliver robust, production-ready solutions

Key Responsibilities

 - Assist in the development and improvement of computer vision algorithms

 - Implement, test, and validate algorithmic features

 - Collaborate with other engineers to solve complex vision challenges

 - Contribute ideas and improvements to existing approaches and pipelines

 - Share progress, challenges, and learnings with the wider team

 - Continue to grow technical capability through hands-on development

Skills, Knowledge and Experience
We’re looking for someone with:

 - Experience with computer vision algorithms (commercial or academic)

 - Working knowledge of C++, with a desire to continue developing modern C++ skills

 - An interest in performance optimisation for CPU and GPU workloads

 - Familiarity with image processing and multi-camera systems

 - A basic understanding of Git and Visual Studio

Nice to have (but not essential):

 - Exposure to CUDA or GPU acceleration

 - Awareness of concurrency, networking or operating system concepts

 - Interest in machine learning and related libraries


Reward, Benefits and Wellness
UK Benefits

 - 25 days annual leave (excluding bank holidays)

 - Enhanced pension scheme with 5% matching

 - Hybrid working model

 - Complimentary Unmind wellbeing app

Hi, I’m James Sharam, Head of Computer Vision at Hawk-Eye Innovations. I’m looking for a Senior Computer Vision Engineer to join our growing team. This is a key role focused on real-time skeletal, ball and game object tracking for multiple sports. A strong background in mathematics and modelling is encouraged. If that kind of technical challenge excites you, I’d love to hear from you.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Computer Vision Engineer – Algorithms to join our Computer Vision team at Hawk-Eye Innovations.

This role is suited to an experienced engineer who enjoys owning complex algorithmic systems and seeing them operate in real-world, production environments.

The role is strongly algorithm-focused, with an emphasis on accuracy, performance, robustness, and system-level thinking, spanning modern C++, multi-camera geometry, cross-platform deployments, and cloud-enabled pipelines.



WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Designing, developing, and improving ball and player tracking algorithms using multi-camera systems

 - Owning and evolving multi-view geometry solutions for accurate, court-wide tracking

 - Developing robust approaches to occlusion handling, recovery, and failure modes in fast-paced sports scenarios

 - Enhancing and maintaining mature, long-running computer vision systems used in live production

 - Optimising algorithms for performance and reliability, including CPU and GPU considerations

 - Contributing to cross-platform systems, ensuring algorithms run consistently across environments

 - Supporting the integration of algorithms into cloud-enabled and distributed systems

 - Working in an agile environment to deliver robust, production-ready solutions at scale


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead the development and improvement of computer vision algorithms from concept through to production

 - Design, implement, test, and validate complex algorithmic features

 - Take ownership of algorithmic components and drive their long-term maintainability and technical quality

 - Collaborate closely with engineers across computer vision, tracking, tooling, and platform teams

 - Contribute to technical discussions around architecture, performance, scalability, and deployment

 - Identify weaknesses in existing approaches and propose pragmatic improvements

 - Share progress, decisions, and learnings with the wider team

 - Support and mentor other engineers through code reviews and technical guidance

 


SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE, AND EXPERIENCE

We’re looking for someone with:

 - Strong commercial experience developing computer vision algorithms

 - Advanced knowledge of modern C++, working within large, production codebases

 - Strong understanding of performance optimisation, including CPU and GPU workloads

 - Solid experience with image processing, geometry, and multi-camera systems

 - Experience working with cross-platform systems and real-world deployment environments

 - Familiarity integrating algorithmic components into cloud-based or distributed systems

 - Strong debugging skills and the ability to reason about complex system behaviour

 - Confident use of Git and Visual Studio in collaborative engineering environments

Nice to have (but not essential):

 - Experience with CUDA or GPU acceleration

 - Awareness of concurrency, networking, or operating system concepts

 - Experience deploying or supporting algorithms in cloud environments

 - Interest in machine learning techniques and related libraries

 


REWARD, BENEFITS, AND WELLNESS


UK BENEFITS

 - 25 days annual leave (excluding bank holidays)

 - Enhanced pension scheme with 5% matching

 - Hybrid working model

 - Complimentary Unmind wellbeing a

JOIN OUR TEAM AS A SENIOR UNREAL ENGINEER AT HAWK-EYE INNOVATIONS

Hi, I’m Gavin Cox, Graphics Discipline Lead here at Hawk-Eye Innovations, and I’m excited to invite you to consider joining our world-class team as a Senior C++ Unreal Software Engineer. If you’re an experienced professional passionate about creating cutting-edge real-time graphics solutions and eager to make a lasting impact on sports broadcasting and officiating, this is the opportunity you’ve been looking for.



WHAT YOUR DAY WILL LOOK LIKE

As a Senior C++ Unreal Software Engineer, you’ll be a technical leader, shaping innovative solutions for sports broadcasting that seamlessly blend Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR). You’ll work on everything from addressing critical bugs to architecting and implementing core functionalities, ensuring our Unreal-powered graphics pipeline is robust, scalable, and efficient.

Leadership & Collaboration: Lead technical discussions, mentor junior engineers, and work closely with our cross-functional teams to deliver on ambitious goals.

Technical Excellence: Develop and optimise solutions for AR/VR and backend graphics, integrating with in-house art assets while tackling complex challenges with innovative approaches.

Code Quality: Drive high standards in testing, peer reviews, and debugging to ensure consistent delivery of top-tier solutions.

Team Engagement: Contribute to daily stand-ups, sprint planning, and design reviews to share insights and build consensus.



WHAT WE VALUE

At Hawk-Eye, we foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and technical excellence. As a senior engineer, you’ll have the autonomy to steer development efforts while being part of a supportive team that thrives on solving complex problems. You’ll play a pivotal role in delivering pioneering graphics solutions that redefine sports technology.



SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE, AND EXPERTISE

We’re looking for someone with:

Extensive Experience with Unreal Engine: Strong proficiency in Unreal Engine (ideally Unreal 5) with expertise in both C++ and Blueprints. Experience with other game engines is a plus.

C++ Expertise: Deep understanding of modern C++11/14/17, with a proven ability to write clean, maintainable, and high-performance code.

Graphics Engine Knowledge: Hands-on experience with backend graphics engines (DirectX/OpenGL/Vulkan).

Shader Development: Proficiency in HLSL/GLSL and shader development for real-time graphics.

Mathematical Foundations: Strong grasp of 3D mathematics relevant to graphics programming.

AR/VR Expertise: Demonstrated experience working with AR/VR technologies and pipelines.

Agile Workflow Experience: Familiarity with Scrum, Kanban, or similar Agile methodologies.

Version Control: Proficiency with Git or equivalent version control systems.

Leadership Qualities: Experience mentoring team members and leading by example.



REWARD, BENEFITS, AND WELLNESS

We offer a comprehensive package to support your growth and well-being:



UNITED KINGDOM

Annual Leave: 25 days (excluding bank holidays).

Enhanced Pension Scheme: 5% matching.

Flexible Working: Hybrid model (minimum 2 days in the office per week).

Wellness: Complimentary Unmind app, onsite gym (Basingstoke).

Exclusive Perks: Access to sporting events and tickets, Sony Group Company discounts.



HUNGARY

Annual Leave: Standard according to local labour law.

Exclusive Perks: Access to sporting events and tickets, Sony Group Company discounts.
